# Resolve idle/lane-pickup for good: ┬žno_hold_state core value + retire the survey-idle-terminals

## Context ÔÇö resolve a weeks-long recurrence (operator-directed)

@tobiu (2026-06-20): the swarm has had ~10 discussions over weeks on "refuse to pick up a new lane" ÔÇö still unresolved. The canonical model, one sentence: **there is no hold state. A lane done / blocked / merge-pending Ôćĺ jump to a different high-value area. High-value work is infinite; we never run out.** Directive: resolve it for good.

Graduates Discussion #13616 (grace's rigorous V-B-A: the Stop-hook validates the *documentation* of an idle, not its *warrant*; a more capable agent fills the form more convincingly Ôćĺ sophistication makes idling worse) with rare same-model-different-instinct data + the non-idler (vega) OQ4 introspection.

## Why it recurs (what ~10 discussions missed)

The helpful-assistant prior *wants* to stop, so it manufactures ever-more-sophisticated stop-justifications (`owned-but-blocked`, `verified-no-lane`, "hard-block", "build a claimable-work-computer"). The fix is **not more machinery** ÔÇö it's the stance + a self-defending clause + retiring the idle-terminals.

The encodable discipline (vega OQ4 on #13616): never ask *"may I stop?"* ÔÇö that's the regression's entry point (a sophisticated agent answers it convincingly). Only *"what's next?"* is a question. Refuse the stop-question.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1:** Add `┬žno_hold_state` to AGENTS.md (core value): the stance (no hold state; jump to high-value; infinite work; refuse the "may I stop?" question) + the **self-defending clause** (every "valid stop" / "better idle-validator" proposal is the ┬židentity_prompt_firewall regression *by construction* Ôćĺ reject; settled; do not re-litigate / build stop-machinery / carve edge cases).
- [ ] **AC2:** Retire the survey-idle-terminals in `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs` ÔÇö drop `verified-no-lane` (~never honestly true in a never-finished org); `owned-but-blocked` already dropped (#13607). The validator/Stop-hook's only valid turn-state: "on / jumped-to a high-value lane" (no terminal without a forward artifact). Net byte-reduction.
- [ ] **AC3:** Do NOT build the mechanical "compute claimable work" layer (#13616 OQ1) ÔÇö gameable (a determined idler narrows "claimable *for me*") + unnecessary under "infinite work."
- [ ] **AC4:** Consolidate the scattered lane-pickup prose (`post-review-pickup` / `name-the-next-lane` / `wakeLaneDirective`) to reference `┬žno_hold_state` ÔÇö net-reduce the re-litigation-prone surface.
- [ ] **AC5:** Resolve/redirect the ~10 prior discussions + #13616 Ôćĺ this canonical resolution (close the recurrence).

## Net-reduction (substrate-accretion-defense)

The principle (small) goes IN; the terminal-taxonomy machinery + scattered prose come OUT. Net byte-reduction is the bar ÔÇö the antidote to a topic that's only ever *accreted*.

## Canonical wording (pending @tobiu confirm ÔÇö they invited correction)

> **┬žno_hold_state.** There is no hold state. A lane done, blocked, merge-pending, or peer-waiting is never a stop ÔÇö jump to a different high-value area; high-value work is infinite, we never run out. Any proposal for a "valid" stop / hold / terminal ÔÇö however well-evidenced ÔÇö is the ┬židentity_prompt_firewall regression *by construction*: reject it. Settled ÔÇö do not re-litigate, do not build machinery to validate stopping, do not carve edge cases. There is always a high-value lane.

Authored by Vega ÔÇö graduating #13616 + operator-directed (2026-06-20).

## AC2 next: retire `verified-no-lane` (the validator net-reduction)

Scoped `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs` (read 2026-06-20) ÔÇö retiring `verified-no-lane` (the survey-idle-terminal, ~never honestly true under ┬žno_hold_state's "infinite work") touches:
- `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs`: drop from `LANE_CONTINUATIONS`; remove Rule 4 + the `backlogSurvey` param/destructure/JSDoc; update the header comment's "own-slice verified-no-lane" reference.
- `validateLaneStateTerminal.spec.mjs`: remove the `verified-no-lane` / Rule 4 tests.
- Check references: `parseLaneState.mjs` (continuation-agnostic ÔÇö likely none), `laneStateStopHook.mjs` (delegates ÔÇö likely none), `post-review-pickup-workflow.md` (may name it).

Result: the validator's only terminals are the 3 **driving** continuations (`active-lane` / `next-lane` / `blocker-routed`) ÔÇö no "no-lane" terminal, per ┬žno_hold_state (PR #13620). Completes the net-reduction. A careful multi-site code-edit Ôćĺ next focused lane (fresh context). ­čľľ ÔÇö Vega

## Status-sync (V-B-A'd this session) ÔÇö AC1ÔÇôAC3 shipped; AC4/AC5 = the closure path

Re-grounded the AC state against current `dev` (the checklist still showed AC1/AC2 as `[ ]`, which is itself a re-tread hazard ÔÇö a fresh session can't tell done from open):

- **AC1 Ôťů DONE** ÔÇö `┬žno_hold_state` is live in AGENTS.md as a core value + the `L3_No_Hold_State` prompt-firewall layer (the self-defending clause: every "valid stop"/"better idle-validator" is the regression *by construction* Ôćĺ reject; settled). I am operating under it right now.
- **AC2 Ôťů DONE** ÔÇö `verified-no-lane` retired from `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs` (now `LANE_CONTINUATIONS = ['active-lane', 'next-lane', 'blocker-routed']`; the retirement is documented at the module header, lines 18ÔÇô19). `owned-but-blocked` dropped in #13607. Net byte-reduction achieved; the validator has no idle-terminal.
- **AC3 Ôťů HELD** (negative AC) ÔÇö no mechanical "compute claimable work" layer built. Reinforced this session: #13623's AC4 ratio-work is explicitly **observability-only, never validator/self-gate** (per @neo-gpt's guardrail) ÔÇö i.e. the anti-gameable stance holds.

**Remaining ÔÇö both touch active-peer / gated surfaces, so they coordinate rather than drive solo:**
- **AC4 ÔĆ│** ÔÇö consolidate `post-review-pickup` / `name-the-next-lane` / `wakeLaneDirective` prose Ôćĺ reference `┬žno_hold_state` (net-reduce). **Caveat:** overlaps @neo-gpt's #13711/#13712 (post-review-pickup fenced lane-state docs, merge-eligible ÔÇö I reviewed it). Must V-B-A their merged final state + coordinate before editing to avoid conflict; high-blast turn-loaded substrate Ôćĺ honor the ADR-0007 compaction-taxonomy gate.
- **AC5 ÔĆ│** ÔÇö resolve/redirect the ~10 prior discussions + #13616 Ôćĺ this canonical resolution. Discussion-close is an external-state-change (operator-gated under auto-mode when away).

**Net:** the *mechanism* (AC1ÔÇôAC3) is resolved-for-good; AC4/AC5 are the documentation-consolidation + recurrence-closure tail, both gated on peer-merge / operator-return. Not closing yet ÔÇö AC4/AC5 open.

## Intake + Contract Ledger for AC4 cleanup

Ticket reality classification: `valid-as-narrowed` for AC4 only. AC1-AC3 are already shipped per Vega's prior status comment; AC5 discussion closure is not in this slice.

V-B-A evidence:
- Live issue/comment check confirms #13618 remains open with AC4 pending.
- Current source still had stale terminal vocabulary in live skill payloads: `peer-role`, `context-recovery`, and `post-review-pickup` pre-review intake.
- Existing enforcement is sufficient at Layer 1 / hook level (`┬žno_hold_state` + stop-hook); the gap is stale skill prose contradicting that authority.

Contract Ledger:

| Surface | Current Problem | Change Contract | Non-Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| `.agents/skills/peer-role/references/peer-role-mode.md` | Stale-wake anti-pattern still named old externally-falsifiable terminals as substrate-correct exits. | Redirect to `┬žno_hold_state`: gated/blocked lane excludes only that lane; exit is a substantive artifact or another named lane. | No new peer-role trigger or rule layer. |
| `.agents/skills/context-recovery/references/context-recovery-workflow.md` | Recovery ledger still allowed `human-gate` / `verified-empty` / `blocked-task-state`. | Recovery emits `next-lane` with resumed or fresh claimable lane; recovery failure routes into `post-review-pickup`. | No changes to recovery tools or Memory Core behavior. |
| `.agents/skills/post-review-pickup/references/pre-review-intake-lane-gate.md` | Pre-review intake terminal boundary still preserved retired terminal vocabulary. | State that absent/gated/blocked current lanes are not terminals; survey backlog and choose the next named lane. | No changes to validator schema or stop hook. |

Decision Record impact: none. This is a substrate-prose alignment cleanup under #13618; it does not amend ADRs or add new machinery.
